claimText: The Basel Convention enforces Prior Informed Consent (PIC) for transboundary hazardous wastes but explicitly excludes radioactive waste.
evidenceExcerpt: Basel Convention (1989) enforces Prior Informed Consent (PIC) for transboundary hazardous wastes, excludes radioactive waste
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope and exclusions of the Basel Convention.
distractorHooks: Applies to all types of waste without exception

claimText: The Rotterdam Convention establishes a legally binding Prior Informed Consent (PIC) procedure for the international trade of certain hazardous chemicals, but it does not outright ban their trade.
evidenceExcerpt: Rotterdam Convention (2004) creates a legally binding PIC procedure for international trade of certain hazardous chemicals/pesticides but does not outright ban them.
examUse: Statement-based question on the regulatory mechanism of the Rotterdam Convention.
distractorHooks: Outright bans the trade of listed hazardous chemicals

claimText: India is a party to all three BRS Conventions: Basel, Rotterdam, and Stockholm.
evidenceExcerpt: India is a party to all three.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's participation in global environmental treaties.
distractorHooks: India is not a party to the Rotterdam Convention
India has only ratified the Stockholm Convention

claimText: Carbosulfan and Fenthion were added to Annex III of the Rotterdam Convention during COP-12.
evidenceExcerpt: Rotterdam COP-12 added Carbosulfan and Fenthion to Annex III.
examUse: Current affairs correct-pair question matching chemicals to their respective conventions.
distractorHooks: Added to the Stockholm Convention as POPs
Added to the Basel Convention as hazardous wastes

claimText: Chlorpyrifos, LC-PFCAs, and MCCPs were added to Annex A of the Stockholm Convention during COP-12.
evidenceExcerpt: Stockholm COP-12 added Chlorpyrifos, LC-PFCAs, and MCCPs to Annex A
examUse: Current affairs statement-based question on newly listed Persistent Organic Pollutants (POPs).
